title: Pruritus without rash

examination.text: Supports documenting onset, duration, localisation, generalisation, time course, intensity, triggers, associated symptoms, medicines, exposures, personal and family history, whole skin, scalp, nails, oral and anogenital examination, and primary versus secondary lesions.
examination.rationale: The exact chronic-pruritus assessment section supports documenting itch without a primary rash, onset, duration, severity and impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, concerns, whole-skin and distribution examination, mucosal context, general appearance and previous records without assigning a cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting onset, duration, localisation, generalisation, time course, intensity, triggers, associated symptoms, medicines, exposures, personal and family history, whole skin, scalp, nails, oral and anogenital examination, and primary versus secondary lesions.

source.title: European Guideline on Chronic Pruritus: In cooperation with the European Dermatology Forum (EDF)

exact_section.heading: Section 5.1 — patient history and clinical examination

examination.text: Supports recording laboratory or other results already available and clinician-selected investigation context according to history and examination; it does not generate a test panel.
examination.rationale: The exact work-up framework supports documenting laboratory or pathology results already available; it does not generate tests.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports recording laboratory or other results already available and clinician-selected investigation context according to history and examination; it does not generate a test panel.

source_limitations: Acute versus chronic classification, exact location and timing, nocturnal pattern, triggers, medicines, systemic review, pregnancy, examination findings, laboratory values, diagnosis, tests, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
